texto: Sometimes asking God for a reason for something is like asking Him why the sky is blue. There is a complex, scientific reason for it, Claire, but most children, including you, are content with knowing it is blue because it is. If we understood everything about everything, we would have no need for faith.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Susan Meissner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Sometimes asking God for a reason for something is like asking Him why the sky is blue. There is …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, faith, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: I used to think mercy meant showing kindness to someone who didn't deserve it, as if only the recipient defined the act. The girl in between has learned that mercy is defined by its giver. texto: ... love and fear can sometimes feel the same, but each will lead a person to take different actions. When a decision h as to be made, fear usually motivates me to choose what is best for me, whereas love motivates me to choose what is best for another person. Fear urges me to hang on, white knuckled, to what is mine, while love can actually lead me to let go.... when you hold something you love tightly to your chest for fear of losing it, you actually risk crushing it against you.
